items.name: NF-κB Signaling
items.summary: NF-κB signaling refers to a family of transcription factor complexes that regulate inflammatory and stress-response gene expression. In many cell types, NF-κB is held inactive in the cytoplasm and becomes activated when upstream signals (such as oxidative stress, cytokines, or microbial products) trigger IκB degradation and nuclear translocation of NF-κB components. Because NF-κB controls broad immune programs, it is frequently implicated in respiratory inflammation and airway remodeling pathways discussed in asthma literature. GENARCH treats NF-κB as a mechanistic bridge that can connect exposure-induced epithelial stress to downstream immune signaling in populations.
